text: **Anna:** Hello!
**Anna:** {People|人々|/ˈpiːpəl/|Many people visit our city.} from all over the world come to Washington, D.
**Anna:** C.
**Anna:** When I'm at work, I love {eating|食べる|/ˈiːtɪŋ/|I am eating lunch now.} lunch outside.
**Anna:** I like to watch people {walking|歩く|/ˈwɔːkɪŋ/|People are walking in the park.} by.
**Anna:** They all look very {different|異なる|/ˈdɪfərənt/|We have different opinions.}.
**Anna:** Today my friend Ashley is eating lunch with me.
**Anna:** Ashley, today the weather is {beautiful|美しい|/ˈbjuːtɪfəl/|What a beautiful day!}, isn't it?
**Ashley:** Yes, it is.
**Ashley:** Ooh, we have to {return|戻る/帰る|/rɪˈtɜːrn/|Let's return home.} to work.
**Anna:** No, we have time.
**Anna:** Let's people watch a little more.
**Ashley:** Okay.
**Anna:** Oh, I know her.
**Anna:** She works in my office.
**Anna:** Kiana, hi.
**Anna:** Come and {join|参加する|/dʒɔɪn/|Please join our team.} us.
**Keyana:** Hi Anna, how are you?
**Anna:** I'm doing great.
**Anna:** Kiana, this is my friend Ashley.
**Keyana:** Hi Ashley.
**Anna:** We need to return to work, but the weather is beautiful and {people watching|人間観察|/ˈpiːpəl wɒtʃɪŋ/|People watching is an interesting hobby.} is fun.
**Keyana:** I love people watching too.
**Anna:** Well, have a seat.
**Anna:** It is fun to see how people are {different|異なる} or the same.
**Anna:** It is.
**Keyana:** For example, Anna, you are {tall|背が高い|/tɔːl/|He is tall and strong.}, but Ashley and I are short.
**Anna:** And Kiana, you and Ashley have brown eyes.
**Anna:** I have blue eyes.
**Keyana:** You two have {light|明るい/軽い|/laɪt/|The room is light and airy.} skin and I have dark skin.
**Anna:** Ashley, you have {straight|まっすぐな/直線的な|/streɪt/|The road is straight.} hair.
**Anna:** Kiana and I have {curly|カール状の/縮れた|/ˈkɜːrli/|She has curly hair.} hair.
**Ashley:** You have very {curly|カール状の/縮れた|/ˈkɜːrli/|Her curly hair is beautiful.} hair, Anna.
**Keyana:** I need to {return|戻る/返却する|/rɪˈtɜːrn/|I will return home soon.} to work.
**Keyana:** See you Anna.
**Keyana:** Nice to meet you, Ashley.
**Ashley:** Bye, Kiana.
**Ashley:** Anna, I have to go too.
**Ashley:** Are you {returning|戻る/帰る|/rɪˈtɜːrnɪŋ/|I am returning from work.} to work?
**Anna:** I still have time and the sun feels so {good|良い/素晴らしい|/ɡʊd/|This food tastes good.}
**Ashley:** Um Anna
**Ashley:** What does your {boss|上司/経営者|/bɒs/|My boss is kind.} look like?
**Anna:** She is {short|低い/背が低い|/ʃɔːrt/|He is short compared to me.}
**Anna:** She has straight {light|明るい/軽い|/laɪt/|The room is light and airy.} hair.
**Ashley:** Does she wear {glasses|眼鏡/めがね|/ˈɡlæsɪz/|She wears glasses every day.}?
**Ashley:** Yes.
**Anna:** Yes she does.
**Ashley:** Is she wearing a blue {sweater|セーター/セーター|/ˈswetər/|I like this warm sweater.} today?
**Anna:** How do you know that?
**Ashley:** She's coming this way.
**Anna:** Oh no, {hide|隠れる/隠す|/haɪd/|Hide behind the door.} me!
**Ashley:** Um bye Anna.
**Ashley:** Call me later.
**Anna:** Bye Ashley.
**Anna:** Talk to you later
**Caty:** Anna, is that you?
**Anna:** Ms.
**Anna:** Weaver, hi.
**Caty:** What are you doing behind that {bench|ベンチ/長椅子|/bentʃ/|Sit on the bench and rest.}?
**Anna:** I am looking for my my stick.
**Anna:** Here it is
**Anna:** I am {people watching|人々を観察する}.
**Anna:** Uh-oh, but it's time to {return|戻る/帰る} to work.
**Caty:** No need to {hurry|急ぐ/急ぐ必要はない}.
**Keyana:** I {love|愛する/大好き} people watching too.
**Caty:** Let's sit.
**Anna:** Sure.
**Caty:** It's a {beautiful|美しい} day, isn't it?
**Ashley:** Yes.
**Anna:** Yes it is Miss Weaver.
**Anna:** People watching in DC is {fun|楽しい/面白い}.
**Anna:** It makes me {forget|忘れる} the time.
**Anna:** Until next time.
